platform_accounts.notes: Use logged-in browser session only when explicitly approved. Prefer extracting job/company info and routing to company career sites. Do not send connection requests or messages without review.

evaluation_context.description: Injected into every Hermes evaluation prompt. Company-specific notes, behavioral guardrails, and whitelist context the LLM should consider before scoring.
behavioral_guardrails: DO NOT skip a job solely because the JD is humorous, quirky, or uses non-literal language. Some companies (especially recruitment agencies and startups) write creative, funny JDs. Evaluate the ROLE, not the writing style.

If a job title contains 'Junior', 'Graduate', 'Entry-Level', or 'Associate', and the JD mentions engineering or AI work (even indirectly), it is NOT a senior role regardless of flowery JD language. Score it against junior/mid criteria.
When a company is known to write quirky JDs (see company_notes), treat the JD text as marketing copy, not a literal checklist. Extract the actual role requirements from context clues.

Python-backend roles that also mention Java: Arinze has Java from his Aptech diploma (Spring, EJB). This is a secondary skill, not a gap. Only treat Java as a gap if it is the PRIMARY language with zero Python overlap.
Arinze's professional experience is ~1.5-2 years, not zero. Vigilis (Aug 2024 - Feb 2026) is real professional experience. Do not score him as if he has no experience.

If a role asks for AI/ML/agent experience, Arinze is a strong candidate: he built Pharmax (AI-integrated SaaS), trained models with HuggingFace/PyTorch, uses Archilles (Hermes Agent fork) daily, and built Haxaml (AI agent governance tool). This is not theoretical — it is hands-on.
Do not inflate scores above 80 unless Arinze genuinely hits most requirements. Being truthful is more important than being optimistic.
Salary: Arinze targets £35,000-£60,000 for junior/mid roles but is flexible for the right opportunity. A role advertising above this range is not a gap unless it requires senior-level experience.

Work authorization: Arinze graduated June 2026 and is on a UK student visa, planning to apply for a Graduate visa (2-year work right). He CAN work in the UK without immediate sponsorship. Only treat sponsorship as a hard blocker if the job explicitly says 'no visa holders' or 'must have permanent right to work.'

scoring_guidance.ai_engineer_roles: Score generously (70+) for AI Engineer, Applied AI, Agentic AI, and ML Engineer roles at junior/mid level. Arinze's FRAME, Haxaml, Pharmax, Archilles, and model training experience directly apply.
scoring_guidance.backend_roles: Score based on Python/FastAPI/PostgreSQL alignment. If Java is secondary, do not penalize heavily. If role is pure Java with no Python, score accordingly lower.
scoring_guidance.fullstack_roles: Arinze is backend-strong. Score 5-10 points lower than pure backend if heavy frontend weighting (React, TypeScript, CSS-in-JS).
scoring_guidance.graduate_roles: Score generously (80+) if the company is a known tech employer and the role is engineering. Arinze's project portfolio (FRAME, Haxaml, Pharmax, CaseFRAME) is strong for a graduate.
scoring_guidance.security_clearance: Automatic skip if SC/DV clearance required. Arinze is not eligible. This is a hard blocker.

mongoose_gray.note: Mongoose Gray is a recruitment agency known for humorous, quirky, and creative job descriptions. The JD text is intentionally playful and should NOT be taken literally. Extracts role requirements from context clues rather than surface-level JD wording. Jobs with titles like 'Junior AI Engineer' or 'Graduate AI Engineer' from Mongoose Gray are REAL engineering roles despite the JD talking about gemstones, cats, ballet, or other whimsical analogies. Evaluate the title and key requirements, not the creative writing.
mongoose_gray.source: user correction — false positive skip of Junior AI Engineer role

experis.note: Experis is a large IT recruitment/staffing agency. Many postings are contract roles (IR35) for enterprise clients. SC clearance is common in Experis postings — check carefully. Do not skip Experis jobs automatically — they post legitimate junior/mid roles alongside senior contracts. Filter based on the actual role requirements, not the agency name.
